body:
## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Identity` **Service directory:** `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/` **Triggered by:** PR #57200 by @m-nash ## What Changed PR #57200 moved all `Azure.Identity` public types into `Azure.Core` and converted `Azure.Identity` into a lightweight `TypeForwardedTo` facade. A `MigrationGuide.md` was added to `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/` covering CS0433 upgrade scenarios. CHANGELOG entries were added but the main **README.md was not updated** to reflect this architectural change. ## Gaps Found - The `README.md` still describes `Azure.Identity` as the home of the credential implementations, with no mention that types now live in `Azure.Core` and are type-forwarded - No link to `MigrationGuide.md` anywhere in the README (e.g. under "Getting started", "Next steps", or a dedicated "Upgrading" section) - The Azure.Core `README.md` key-concepts section lists `TokenCredentials` as an abstraction but never mentions that `DefaultAzureCredential` and all other credential types are now bundled in `Azure.Core` itself — users who read only the Azure.Core README have no way to discover this - No note in the `Azure.Identity` README that consumers of `Azure.Core` 1.53+ no longer need a separate `Azure.Identity` package reference to use credentials
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1: `sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/README.md` — **edit** Add a callout near the top of the README (after the header links, before or at the start of **Getting started**) to inform users about the TypeForwarding change and point them to the migration guide: ```markdown > **Note (v1.53.0+):** Starting with version 1.53.0, all `Azure.Identity` types have been consolidated into `Azure.Core` and are forwarded from this package using [`TypeForwardedTo`](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/api/system.runtime.compilerservices.typeforwardedtoattribute). Existing code continues to compile and run without changes. If you encounter [CS0433](https://learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/csharp/language-reference/compiler-messages/cs0433) errors after upgrading, see the [Migration Guide](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/MigrationGuide.md). ``` Additionally, add a link in the **Next steps** section: ```markdown - [Migration guide for Azure.Identity type consolidation into Azure.Core](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/MigrationGuide.md) ``` #### File 2: `sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md` — **edit** In the **Key concepts** section, update the bullet describing credential abstractions to note that credential implementations are now bundled in Azure.Core itself: Replace: ```markdown - Abstractions for representing Azure SDK credentials. (`TokenCredentials`). ``` With: ```markdown - Abstractions and implementations for authenticating Azure SDK clients (`TokenCredential`, `DefaultAzureCredential`, and all other credential types from `Azure.Identity` are now included in `Azure.Core` starting with version 1.53.0 — no separate `Azure.Identity` package reference is needed). ``` Also add a note to the **Getting started** section: ```markdown ### Authentication Starting with `Azure.Core` 1.53.0, all authentication credential types (such as `DefaultAzureCredential`, `ClientSecretCredential`, `ManagedIdentityCredential`, etc.) are bundled directly in this package. A separate `Azure.Identity` reference is no longer required. If you are upgrading and encounter CS0433 errors, see the [Azure.Identity migration guide](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/MigrationGuide.md). ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new snippet test files are required for these prose-only README additions. The existing credential usage snippets (e.g., `DefaultAzureCredential` in `sdk/core/Azure.Core/tests/`) already demonstrate credential usage. ### Step 3: Verify README structure Both READMEs already contain the required sections (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing). No structural additions are needed — only content updates within existing sections. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 identity` 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 identity` 5. `dotnet format sdk/identity/Azure.Identity/` And for Azure.Core: 1. `dotnet build sdk/core/Azure.Core/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/core/Azure.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 core`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above
title: [Identity] Docs: Update README to reflect TypeForwarding facade architecture in 1.53.0
